notes.eclipse_type_mapping: CSV uses two columns (type + subtype): 'Solar','Total+Central' → DB type='solar_total'. Subtype 'Annular-Total+Central' (15 rows) maps to 'solar_annular' — the DB uses the primary eclipse class, dropping the '+Central'/'+Non-Central' and '-Total' qualifiers. All 913 rows verified by date+sign+nakshatra+longitude.
notes.retrograde_schema: Each CSV row (one retrograde period) maps to TWO DB rows: station_type='retrograde_start' (station_retro_date + sign_at_station_retro + deg_at_station_retro) and station_type='retrograde_end' (station_direct_date + sign_at_station_direct + deg_at_station_direct). CSV 1231 rows → DB 2462 rows is the correct expected ratio.
notes.retrograde_nakshatra: nakshatra column is NULL for all 2462 retrograde rows. CSV source does not include nakshatra data for retrograde stations — this is expected schema gap, not a migration defect. arc_retrograded (CSV) is not persisted to DB — also expected, as the DB schema does not include this column.
notes.eclipse_subtype: CSV 'subtype' column (e.g. 'Total+Central', 'Annular+Non-Central') is not preserved in DB — DB stores only the primary type class. This is a deliberate schema simplification. No data loss for sign/nakshatra/longitude.

retrogrades.null_nakshatra_note: CSV source contains no nakshatra data for retrograde stations — 100% NULL is expected, not a defect
retrogrades.arc_retrograded_note: arc_retrograded column not in DB schema — deliberate omission, source data not lost (CSV retained)
